case_summary: Slow ceiling stain under a relatively new geyser — points at a fitting or flexi rather than tank corrosion.

ground_truth.notes: Contrasts with the corroded-tank case — the geyser is young, the leak is localised to a fitting, and the water is clear (no rust). Tests whether the system correctly picks fitting-leak over corroded-tank when cues do NOT align with corrosion.

inputs.user_text: We've got a damp patch on the ceiling under our geyser that's slowly getting bigger. The water in the drip tray is clear, no discolouration. The geyser was installed about 3 years ago. The drip seems to be coming from one of the connections at the top of the geyser rather than from the tank itself.
